title: Smoky Zesty Malaysian Dissolved Wrap
description: A smoky and zesty Malaysian-inspired dish featuring coconut sugar, steamed in a leaf wrap, perfect for a brunch mood. This dish is kosher-friendly and served in a scallop shell.

ingredients.name: Scallop Shell Wraps
ingredients.notes: available at Asian markets or online

instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped lemongrass, grated ginger, and grated galangal to the skillet and saut9 for 2 minutes.
Add the coconut milk to the skillet and bring to a simmer.
Add the fish sauce, sambal oelek, and chopped kaffir lime leaves to the skillet and stir to combine.
Arrange the coconut sugar in the center of a scallop shell wrap, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Pour the coconut milk mixture over the coconut sugar, making sure to stay within the border.
Fold the leaves of the scallop shell wrap over the filling and secure with a toothpick.
Serve immediately, garnished with chopped scallions and a sprinkle of toasted coconut flakes.
Cook the scallop shell wraps according to package instructions for steaming.
Steam the wraps for 2-3 minutes, or until they are softened and pliable.
Remove the wraps from the steamer and fill each one with the coconut milk mixture, using about 1/2 cup of filling for each wrap.
Garnish with chopped scallions and a sprinkle of toasted coconut flakes before serving.
